# Architectural Drawing Description

This is a historical architectural blueprint on aged parchment paper featuring detailed cross-sectional drawings of a Victorian-era mansion. The main composition displays a longitudinal section view in the center-lower portion of the page, showing the building's full side profile with multiple stories, pitched roofs with decorative ridges, windows, doors, and internal architectural details with dimensional notations. On the left side is a detailed elevation of a tall, conical tower or cupola with vertical ribbing and internal structural framework. The upper right contains a smaller cross-sectional view labeled "Transverse Section," depicting the building's depth and internal room arrangements with multiple roof levels. Handwritten notes and measurements appear throughout in brown ink, including labels such as "Longitudinal Section," "D. Revell & Symonds," and various dimension markers. A color reference bar appears along the right edge of the document. The overall drawing is executed in brown and black ink lines on a beige, aged paper background showing typical weathering and aging marks consistent with historical architectural documents.

# Architectural Floor Plan Description

This is a historical architectural blueprint showing the second floor layout of a building designed by F. McIlvougall & Son, Architects, located at 530 Pine Street. The document is aged parchment with visible staining and deterioration throughout. At the top is a color reference scale bar containing rectangles of various colors including dark blue, cyan, green, yellow, magenta, pink, white, and dark gray, accompanied by a measurement ruler.

The floor plan employs tan and brown line work to delineate room boundaries and structural elements. The layout displays multiple rooms with their dimensions marked in feet and inches. Notable labeled spaces include two "Bedroom" areas (each measuring approximately 13'6" × 16'), "Bed Rooms," "Halls," "Bath Rooms," and various other residential spaces. The upper portion features two symmetrical "Bedroom Dressing" areas with dimensions of 16'6" × 19'6". Diagonal lines within rooms indicate ceiling elements or architectural details. The plan includes staircase representations shown with geometric patterns, doorways marked with swing indicators, and structural notation symbols throughout. At the bottom, text reads "SECOND FLOOR" and "SCALED: EIGHT FEET TO ONE INCH," establishing the measurement scale for the drawing.

# Architectural Floor Plan Description

This is a historical architectural blueprint for the first floor of a residential building, drawn on aged parchment paper with visible stains and creasing. At the top of the document is a color reference scale bar showing various colors from dark blue through cyan, green, yellow, magenta, white, and gray. The main drawing uses light brown ink lines to delineate room layouts and dimensions marked throughout in feet and inches.

The floor plan displays a symmetrical layout with a central corridor system. On the upper portion are residential quarters including a "Stair Room" (marked 8'x25'), a "Kitchen" (15'x15'8"), and various smaller rooms including closets and a room marked "Pantry." The middle section contains two large formal spaces: "Old Ladies Dining Room" (30'x30') on the left and "Children's Dining Room" (30'x30') on the right, separated by a central hall passage with "Children's Entrance" noted below.

The lower section shows additional bedroom accommodations with multiple "Bed Room" spaces, a "Hall" on each side, "Sitting Rooms," and central facilities including a "Vertique" (likely a vertical element) and "Dressing Room." At the bottom, architectural notation reads "SCALE: EIGHT FEET TO ONE INCH" and credits "B. M. HOUGHSALL & SON, ARCHTS., 330 PINE STR. S.F." The document is labeled "FIRST FLOOR" at the bottom center.
